name: Asking for Help
description: Know when and how to ask a grown-up or friend for help

name: Calm-Down Strategies
description: Practice breathing, counting to 5, or squeeze and release

name: Expressing Needs
description: Use words to say what you need

name: Following Routines
description: Understand and follow simple daily routines

name: Kindness Actions
description: Identify kind actions like sharing, comforting, and helping

name: Naming Emotions
description: Identify and name basic emotions

name: Recognizing Emotions in Others
description: Look at faces and body language to tell how someone feels

name: Taking Turns
description: Practice waiting and sharing through structured games

name: Color & Mood
description: Identify how colors can express emotions and moods in artwork

name: Famous Artists
description: Recognize the styles of famous artists (Mondrian, Kandinsky) and describe how their art makes us feel

name: Pattern & Repetition
description: Recognize, create, and extend repeating patterns (AB, ABB, ABC) using visual elements in art

name: Pixel Art Creation
description: Create simple images using a pixel grid, understanding that digital images are made of tiny colored squares

name: Color Mixing
description: Discover what happens when two colors mix

name: Color Naming
description: Identify and name primary and secondary colors

name: Free Drawing
description: Express ideas through open-ended drawing

name: Pattern Making
description: Create simple visual patterns with stamps or shapes

name: Shape Drawing
description: Draw or trace circles, lines, and simple shapes

name: Texture Awareness
description: Describe textures (smooth, rough, bumpy, soft)

name: Self-Expression Through Art
description: Use artistic choices (color, composition, subject) to express personal ideas and interests in original artwork

name: Texture
description: Identify and describe a variety of textures in artwork and the real world, including rough, smooth, bumpy, and soft

name: Addition/Subtraction within 1000
description: Add and subtract within 1000 using models and strategies based on place value

name: Arrays and Repeated Addition
description: Use rectangular arrays to model repeated addition and understand rows and columns

name: Coin Identification
description: Identify pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters and their values

name: Comparing 3-Digit Numbers
description: Compare two three-digit numbers using >, =, and < symbols

name: Data & Graphs
description: Read and interpret data from picture graphs and bar graphs with up to four categories

name: Multi-Step Word Problems
description: Solve one-step and two-step word problems using addition and subtraction within 100

name: Place Value to 1000
description: Understand that the three digits of a three-digit number represent hundreds, tens, and ones

name: More and Less
description: Compare groups to identify which has more or fewer

name: Number Recognition 1-5
description: Recognize written numerals 1 through 5

name: One-to-One Counting
description: Touch and count objects up to 5 (age 3) or up to 10 (age 4)

name: Rote Counting to 10
description: Count aloud from 1 to 10 in sequence

name: Shape Recognition
description: Identify circles, squares, and triangles

name: Simple Patterns
description: Identify and extend AB patterns

name: Size Comparison
description: Compare big/small, tall/short, long/short

name: Sorting by Attribute
description: Sort objects by one attribute (color, shape, or size)

name: Telling Time to 5 Minutes
description: Tell and write time to the nearest five minutes using analog and digital clocks

name: Melody & Pitch
description: Identify high and low pitches, sing and notate simple solfege patterns (Do Re Mi Fa Sol)

name: Music+Math Connections
description: Connect musical note values to fractions and addition, understanding that beats can be counted and divided mathematically

name: Musical Form (AB, ABA)
description: Recognize and describe contrasting sections (AB) and return-of-A forms (ABA) in simple songs

name: Performance & Expression
description: Combine tempo, dynamics, and musical feeling to perform with expression and communicate emotion

name: Fast and Slow
description: Distinguish between fast and slow rhythms

name: Keeping a Beat
description: Clap, stomp, or tap along to a steady beat

name: Loud and Soft
description: Distinguish between loud and quiet sounds

name: Musical Movement
description: Move body to music

name: Singing Along
description: Sing simple songs and nursery rhymes

name: Sound Exploration
description: Identify everyday sounds

name: Rhythm Patterns
description: Create, perform, and notate simple rhythm patterns using quarter notes, eighth notes, half notes, and rests

name: Animation
description: Use loops and timing to create simple animations and repeating visual patterns on a device display

name: Conditionals (If-Then)
description: Use if-then and if-else logic to make programs choose between different actions

name: Debugging
description: Find and fix mistakes in code by reading carefully and testing step by step

name: Display Text & Graphics
description: Use code to show text, pictures, and colors on a screen

name: Events (Button Input)
description: Understand that pressing a button triggers an event that makes something happen in a program

name: Loops (Repeat)
description: Understand that loops repeat a set of instructions multiple times to avoid writing the same code over and over

name: Cause and Effect
description: Predict what a button press will do

name: Matching Input to Output
description: Connect an action to its result

name: Problem Decomposition
description: Break a task into 2 simple steps

name: Repeating Patterns
description: Identify when something repeats

name: Sequencing
description: Put 2 to 3 picture cards in the right order

name: Simple Directions
description: Give and follow directional instructions

name: Sensor Input (IMU)
description: Use tilt and motion sensor data as input to control program behavior

name: Sequences & Order
description: Arrange instructions in the correct order so a program runs properly

name: Sound & Buzzer
description: Use buzzer and sound commands to create audio feedback in programs

name: Variables (Data Storage)
description: Understand that variables are named containers that store and update information in a program

name: Habitats & Biodiversity
description: Compare different habitats and explain how animals are suited to where they live

name: Landforms & Water Bodies
description: Identify and describe common landforms and bodies of water including mountains, valleys, plains, rivers, lakes, and oceans

name: Animal Sounds and Homes
description: Match animals to their sounds and habitats

name: Cause and Effect
description: Predict what happens when you do something

name: Living vs. Non-Living
description: Sort things into living and non-living

name: Nature Observation
description: Observe and describe plants, animals, and weather

name: Plant Growth
description: Understand that plants need water and light to grow

name: Sink or Float
description: Predict and test whether objects sink or float

name: Using Senses
description: Describe objects using the five senses

name: Weather Awareness
description: Describe today's weather

name: Push and Pull Forces
description: Investigate how pushes, pulls, friction, gravity, and simple machines affect the motion of objects

name: Seeds & Pollination
description: Describe how seeds are dispersed and how pollination helps plants reproduce

name: Slow & Fast Earth Changes
description: Compare events that happen slowly (erosion, weathering) with events that happen quickly (earthquakes, volcanoes)

name: What Plants Need
description: Identify what plants need to grow: sunlight, water, air, and nutrients from soil

name: Parts of Speech
description: Identify and use nouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in sentences

name: Beginning Sound Awareness
description: Identify the first sound in a word

name: Book Awareness
description: Hold a book correctly; understand front/back, top/bottom

name: Letter Recognition (A-Z)
description: Recognize uppercase letters by sight

name: Listening Comprehension
description: Answer simple questions about a read-aloud story

name: Name Recognition
description: Recognize own written name

name: Rhyme Recognition
description: Identify words that rhyme

name: Syllable Awareness
description: Clap out syllables in familiar words

name: Vocabulary Building
description: Learn new words through context and pictures

name: Punctuation & Capitalization
description: Use correct capitalization, commas, and end punctuation in writing

name: Reading Comprehension (Informational)
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in an informational text

name: Data & Graphs
description: Read and interpret picture graphs and bar graphs to answer questions

name: Estimation & Reasonableness
description: Use estimation to check whether answers make sense and are reasonable

name: Logic Puzzles
description: Use if-then reasoning and elimination to solve clue-based puzzles

name: Pattern Extension
description: Identify, extend, and apply rules to complete visual and number patterns

name: AB Patterns
description: Recognize and extend alternating patterns

name: Following Directions
description: Follow 2-step verbal instructions in order

name: Odd One Out
description: Identify which item does not belong in a group

name: Shape Sorting
description: Sort objects by shape, then by color, then by size

name: Simple Puzzles
description: Complete 4 to 8 piece puzzles

name: What Comes Next
description: Predict the next item in a sequence

name: Problem Decomposition
description: Break a large problem or task into smaller, manageable steps
